subject: Ecology
title: Soil Biodiversity Hotspots
description: A fast breakdown of Soil Biodiversity Hotspots for builders.

hook.text: A handful of forest soil contains more species than the entire above-ground rainforest canopy.

buildup.text: Bacteria, archaea, fungi, protists, nematodes, mites, and insects share every cubic centimeter.

discovery.text: Most soil species haven't been named yet—science has cataloged less than 1% of soil microbes.

twist.text: Losing soil biodiversity can collapse nutrient cycling, disease suppression, and water filtration.

climax.text: Soil biodiversity is the invisible foundation that holds visible ecosystems together.

punchline.text: We know more about Mars than our own soil.

quiz.question: What percentage of soil microbes has science cataloged?
